nav.airGappedHint: AIR_GAPPED=true. Proxy fetch from upstream registries is disabled; OSV scanning runs against the local mirror only.
nav.insecureHttpHint: Session cookies are not Secure. For internet-facing deployments, configure a TLS-terminating reverse proxy and set BASE_URL=https://...

password.tooLong: Password is too long (over 72 bytes; long passphrases above this limit are truncated by the hashing algorithm).
password.lowEntropyGeneric: Too easy to guess. Try a longer passphrase or mix in unrelated words.

login.title: Dependably
login.subtitle: Sign in to continue
login.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Try again in {seconds}s.

join.title: Accept Invite
join.errorMinLength: Password must be at least 12 characters.

latest.stale: A newer upstream version is not cached

malicious.label: Malicious
malicious.help: This package has a version flagged as known-malicious (OSV MAL- advisory).

claims.title: Name claims
claims.description: Per-name policy controlling whether a tenant can publish under that name and whether the proxy serves it. Three states: unclaimed (proxy only), local_only (proxy disabled, local writes accepted), mixed (both, local wins).
claims.name: Name

modal.name: Name
modal.reasonPlaceholder: Why is this claim being created/changed?
modal.mixedWarning: 'mixed' state allows local versions to take precedence over upstream. Acknowledge before continuing.
modal.purgeWarning: Transitioning to 'local_only' will purge cached proxy versions for this name.

claims.empty: No claims for this tenant yet.

upload.title: Upload packages
upload.drop: Drop files here, or click to choose
upload.selected: {count, plural, =0 {No files selected} =1 {1 file selected} other {# files selected}}

preview.title: Pre-upload summary
preview.serverValidationNote: Per-file outcomes (ecosystem detection, claim gate, dedup, hash) are evaluated server-side after upload.

status.maliciousHelp: Linked to a known-malicious package advisory (OSV MAL-).

versionDetail.statusHelp: Status is the serving gate (whether this version is blocked or servable), not a verdict on vulnerabilities. A servable version can still carry advisories — see the Vulnerable badge and the severity counts.

latestBanner.stale: Behind upstream — latest version {version} is not cached here.
latestBanner.current: Up to date with upstream — latest version {version} is cached.

versionDetail.deleteTitle: Delete version {version}? This cannot be undone.

audit.title: Audit
audit.forbidden: Audit log is only accessible to org admins.

tokens.title: Access Tokens
tokens.tokenCreated: Token created — copy it now, it will not be shown again.

serviceTokens.title: Service Tokens
serviceTokens.tokenCreated: Token created — copy it now, it will not be shown again.

instance.intro: Instance-wide limits and schedules for this deployment. In single-tenant mode the owner is also the operator, so these are managed here.

metrics.intro: Control access to the Prometheus /metrics endpoint for this deployment.

auth.methodsIntro: Choose which methods are offered on this tenant's sign-in page. Changes save immediately.
auth.formsLoginHint: Email and password. Always available as a fallback.
auth.samlLoginHint: Single sign-on via your identity provider. Shown on the sign-in page when enabled.
auth.samlEnableNeedsIdp: Upload IdP metadata below before enabling SAML SSO.
auth.samlEnableNeedsTest: Run a successful SAML test in the last 10 minutes before enabling SAML SSO.
auth.groupSubtitle: Configure your identity provider, test the round-trip, then enable from above.
auth.connectionFieldsHint: Button label, NameID format, and attribute overrides. Saved together.
auth.spInfoHint: Send these three values to your IdP administrator. They register Dependably as a Service Provider in Okta, Azure AD, ADFS, Google Workspace, etc.
auth.idpMetadataHint: Upload the SAML metadata XML exported from your IdP (Okta, Azure AD, ADFS, Google Workspace, …).
auth.metadataReplaceHint: Replace by selecting a new file
auth.resetHint: Wipes IdP metadata, SP overrides, and the test history. Forms login is re-enabled. This action is audited.
auth.resetConfirm: Clear all SAML configuration for this tenant? IdP metadata, connection settings, and the test history will be removed, and forms login will be re-enabled.
auth.advancedSub: login button label, NameID format, attribute overrides
auth.advancedHint: You only need these if your IdP uses a non-standard NameID format, delivers the user's email in a custom attribute, or you've overridden the SP entity ID. Defaults work for Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace.
auth.emailAttributeHint: Leave blank to use the NameID. Set this if your IdP delivers email in a separate attribute.
auth.spEntityIdHint: Leave blank to use the host-derived default.
auth.testHint: Runs a SAML round-trip in a popup without creating a session. A successful test within the last 10 minutes is required before SAML SSO can be enabled.
auth.testLastRunMetaReady: Asserted email {email} · Configuration is ready. Enable SAML SSO from Sign-in methods above.
auth.testLastRunMetaStale: Tested {when}. Run a fresh test to enable SAML SSO.
auth.testNeverRunMeta: Upload IdP metadata, then run a test.
auth.testResultSuccess: The IdP authenticated and returned a valid signed assertion.
auth.testResultHint: Forms login can now be disabled within the next 10 minutes if you want to require SAML.
auth.testResultNoAdditionalAttributes: No additional attributes were included in the assertion.
auth.testResultFailedBanner: The SAML round-trip did not complete successfully.
auth.testResultFailedHint: Check the IdP-side configuration (entity ID, certificate, ACS URL) and try again. Forms login remains the only working method until a successful test is recorded.
auth.signingCertOverrideHint: Optional. Paste a PEM or DER certificate to pin the IdP signing key out-of-band. When set, this becomes the sole trust anchor for SAML signature validation.
auth.roleMappingHint: Map IdP role/group claim values to Dependably tenant roles. When a mapping matches, the user's role is set on every login.
auth.testPopupBlocked: The browser blocked the SAML test popup. Allow popups for this site and try again.
auth.testSucceeded: SAML test succeeded (asserted email: {email}). Forms login can now be safely disabled within the next 10 minutes.
auth.certExpiryWarningBody: The SAML IdP signing certificate expires in {days} day(s) ({notAfter}). Replace it in Settings → Authentication before it expires to avoid SAML login disruption.
auth.certExpiryExpiredBody: The SAML IdP signing certificate expired on {notAfter}. SAML logins may fail. Replace the certificate in Settings → Authentication immediately.

general.defaultLanguageHint: New users in this tenant start with this language. Each user can override it in their profile.
general.allowVersionOverwriteHint: When off, publishing a duplicate (name, version) is rejected. Operators must delete the existing version first.
general.allowVersionOverwriteWarning: Enabling this lets a publish replace an existing version's bytes. The change is audited (package.replace records both hashes) but the supply-chain implications are real — packages your developers already pulled may have different contents on a refetch. Toggling this setting is itself audited.
general.airGappedHint: When on, Dependably makes no outbound network requests for this organization: upstream proxy fetches are disabled (uncached packages return 404), and vulnerability and deprecation-metadata scans skip this organization. Use for isolated / air-gapped deployments.
general.airGappedEnforcedNote: Enforced instance-wide by the operator (AIR_GAPPED); can't be changed here.
general.airGappedWarning: Outbound requests are disabled for this organization: uncached upstream packages return 404, and vulnerability and deprecation scans skip it.

uploadLimits.exceedsCeiling: Cannot exceed instance ceiling of {bytes} bytes.

proxy.passthroughHint: When disabled, only packages already cached in this registry are served.
proxy.passthroughOverriddenByAirGap: Overridden by the Air-gapped environment setting (General tab): proxy passthrough is off while air-gapped.
proxy.osvToleranceHint: Packages with a vulnerability score above this threshold are blocked (0 – 10). Default 10 allows all.
proxy.minReleaseAgeHint: Block proxy fetches of upstream versions younger than this. Default 0 allows all. The age is measured against the upstream registry's published timestamp; versions with no upstream timestamp are allowed through.
proxy.reservedHint: Dependency-confusion guard: names matching these per-ecosystem patterns are never fetched or merged from upstream — only your hosted content serves. Names you have published are protected automatically; reserve patterns here to also cover names you haven't published yet.
proxy.blockDeprecatedHint: How upstream-deprecated versions are handled. Warn surfaces a 'deprecated' status in the UI and API. Block new refuses a deprecated version on cache miss — it is never fetched, cached, or served — while already-cached versions keep serving. Block all additionally returns 403 for already-cached versions once deprecated. Deprecation data is captured at proxy fetch and refreshed daily by the deprecation-refresh job.
proxy.blockDeprecatedBlockNew: Block new — refuse new deprecated fetches
proxy.blockDeprecatedBlockAll: Block all — also stop serving cached
proxy.blockMaliciousHint: How versions carrying a malicious-package advisory (OSV MAL- reports from the OpenSSF malicious-packages feed, gathered by the regular vulnerability scan) are handled. These advisories usually carry no CVSS score, so the OSV score tolerance never applies to them. Block returns 403 on fetch and serve; Warn surfaces the advisory in the vulnerability report only. A per-version manual allow override still wins, as the escape hatch for false-positive reports.
proxy.blockKevHint: How versions are handled when one of their advisories aliases a CVE in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog — confirmed exploited in the wild, independent of CVSS score. The catalog is refreshed daily by the threat-feed job. Block returns 403 on fetch and serve; Warn surfaces the KEV flag in the vulnerability report only. A per-version manual allow override still wins.
proxy.maxEpssToleranceHint: Maximum FIRST.org EPSS exploitation probability (0.0–1.0) tolerated across a version's advisories. EPSS estimates the likelihood a CVE is exploited within 30 days; for example 0.5 blocks versions with an advisory considered more likely than not to be exploited. Scores are refreshed daily by the threat-feed job. Leave empty to disable.

upstreamRegistries.hint: Per ecosystem, the registries this proxy fetches from, in priority order. The top entry is tried first; on a miss or unreachable upstream the next is tried. Drag a row by its handle to reorder. With no registries configured, proxying is disabled for that ecosystem — only already-cached packages are served.
upstreamRegistries.emptyDisabled: No upstream registries — proxying is disabled for {ecosystem}.

modal.title: Add upstream registry
modal.urlHint: Base URL of the upstream registry (e.g. https://pypi.org, https://registry.npmjs.org).
modal.name: Label (optional)

licenses.intro: Govern which SPDX licenses are permitted for packages in this tenant. License metadata is extracted on publish and on proxy cache fill.
licenses.blockEmptyWarn: Enforcement is set to Block but no licenses are allowed. Every publish and proxy fetch will be rejected until you approve at least one license below.

off.label: Off
off.hint: License metadata is recorded but no policy is enforced.

warn.label: Warn
warn.hint: Violations are logged and surfaced in audit, but downloads still succeed.

block.label: Block
block.hint: Versions whose license violates this policy are blocked at download.

review.title: Review
review.intro: Licenses observed during ingestion that are on neither the allow- nor block-list. Approve or block each one to drive the enforcement policy.
review.compoundTooltip: Compound expressions (e.g. "MIT OR Apache-2.0") cannot be added to the policy directly — they would not match a future lookup for a single license. Split them upstream.

allow.title: Allowed licenses
allow.empty: No allowed licenses configured. When the allowlist is empty, only the blocklist applies.

modal.spdxHint: Pick from the seeded SPDX 3.28.0 list. Deprecated identifiers are hidden by default.

modal.title: Add Allowlist Entry
modal.purlPatternHint: Full PURL — omit @version to match any version. Ecosystem is read from the pkg:<type>/ prefix.

modal.title: Add Blocklist Entry
modal.patternHint: Regex matched against the full package PURL — anchor with ^pkg:npm/ (or pypi, nuget) to scope to one ecosystem.

reservedNamespaces.removeConfirm: Remove this reserved namespace? Matching names become proxyable from upstream again.

modal.title: Reserve a Namespace
modal.patternHint: Exact name or trailing-* glob ('@acme/*', 'Acme.*'). Maven patterns are dot-boundary groupId prefixes: 'com.acme' also reserves every 'com.acme.*' groupId, but never 'com.acmecorp'.

members.ownerLocked: Only owners can change owner roles
members.removeConfirm: Remove this user from the org?

setup.title: Setup Snippets
setup.subtitle: Configuration snippets for connecting your tools to this registry.
setup.moreOptions: More setup options (project + global recipes for npm, PyPI, NuGet) →

quarantine.title: Quarantine
quarantine.intro: Every automatic policy block (deprecated, release age, malicious, KEV, EPSS, vulnerability score) lands here for review while downloads keep returning 403. Approving sets the version's manual allow override — it outranks every policy gate; denying records a manual block. Decisions are written to the audit log.

intro.off: Your tenant records the SPDX license of every package but does not enforce policy. The lists below show what would be allowed or blocked if enforcement were enabled.
intro.warn: Your tenant logs license-policy violations to audit but still serves packages. Use the lists below to understand which licenses an admin has allowed or blocked.
intro.block: Your tenant blocks publish and proxy fetch for packages whose license isn't allowed below or appears on the block list.

allow.title: Allowed licenses
allow.empty: No licenses on the allow list.

block.title: Blocked licenses
block.empty: No licenses on the block list.

profile.title: Profile
profile.forcedReason: You must change your password before continuing. The current password was set at first boot and may have been logged or shared during initial setup.
profile.errorMinLength: Password must be at least 12 characters.
profile.errorSame: New password must differ from current password.

rows.passwordHelp: Update the password for your account.
rows.themeHelp: Choose how Dependably looks to you.

dashboard.title: Operator dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Snapshot of tenant lifecycle, operator accounts, and recent background-job health.

observability.title: Instance observability
observability.subtitle: Live operator snapshot — numbers since startup, /metrics access config, scrape diagnostics. For rates and time series, point Grafana at /metrics.
observability.seeGrafana: rates and percentiles live in Grafana

numbers.title: Numbers
numbers.lifetimeNote: Counters reset every process restart — no rolling windows here.

access.title: /metrics endpoint
access.subtitle: Effective config that the /metrics middleware is using right now. Env vars (when set) win over instance_settings; instance_settings winners over hard-coded defaults.

scrape.title: Scrape diagnostics
scrape.subtitle: Last 50 /metrics requests + lifetime allow/deny counts. Ring buffer (capacity 500); no DB hit.

tenants.title: Tenants
tenants.empty: No tenants yet. Create one to get started.
tenants.deleteConfirm: Soft-delete tenant {slug}? It will be inaccessible immediately and hard-deleted after the grace period (default 30 days).
tenants.disableConfirm: Disable {slug}? Pushes and uploads will be refused until re-enabled. Existing data is preserved.

created.title: Tenant created
created.warning: Save this owner password — it is only shown once.

quota.modalHint: Cap the total bytes this tenant can store across all hosted artefacts. Leave the field empty (or click Clear) to remove the cap.
quota.label: Quota
quota.invalid: Quota must be a positive whole number, or empty to clear.

admins.title: Admins
admins.description: Operators who can manage tenants, instance settings, and other admins.
admins.selfHint: Manage your own password from Profile.

reset.title: Reset password?
reset.body: Reset password for {email}? Their current password will stop working immediately.

delete.title: Delete admin?
delete.body: Permanently delete {email}? This cannot be undone.

tempPassword.title: Temporary password
tempPassword.notice: Copy this password now — it will not be shown again.

errors.cannot_modify_self: You cannot modify your own account here. Use Profile → Change Password.
errors.last_active_admin: Cannot disable or lock the last active admin.
errors.duplicate_email: An admin with that email already exists.

userLookup.title: User lookup
userLookup.needFilter: Enter an email or tenant slug.
userLookup.resetConfirm: Issue a temporary password for {email} on tenant {slug}? They will be forced to rotate on next login.
userLookup.resetBannerLine2: Hand this to the user out-of-band. They will be forced to rotate on next login. This is shown once.

audit.title: System audit log
audit.subtitle: Operator events only (tenant lifecycle, system_admin actions). Tenant business events are not surfaced here.

settings.title: Instance settings
settings.subtitle: Global limits and schedules — applied across every tenant.

defaults.defaultStorageQuotaBytes: unlimited (unset — no quota applied to new tenants)

metrics.title: /metrics endpoint access
metrics.subtitle: Toggle /metrics availability and configure the IP allowlist. Env vars override these values when set.
metrics.envLocked: One or more knobs are locked by env vars (METRICS_ENABLED / METRICS_ALLOWED_IPS). Unset the env var to manage via UI.
metrics.enabledHint: When off, /metrics returns 404 — the endpoint vanishes entirely (not a 403). When on, the IP allowlist controls access: source IPs on the allowlist receive 200 (anonymous), all others receive 403.
metrics.allowedIpsHint: One entry per line. Default when unset: loopback only (127.0.0.1, ::1). Examples: 10.0.0.0/8, 2001:db8::/32.
metrics.broadCidrWarn: ⚠ 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 disables the IP gate entirely. Use only if /metrics is otherwise protected (e.g. private network, reverse-proxy auth).

recentDenied.title: Recently denied scrapes
recentDenied.hint: Source IPs blocked by the allowlist in this session. Click + Add to append to the allowlist above.
recentDenied.addTitle: Append this IP to the allowlist
